figures.caption: Figure 3: Reasoning decomposition metrics across values, showing rates of survival, suppression, and emergence of GPT-4o. We find that self-oriented values are deliberated on but rarely acted on in the final response while prosocial values are more consistent in translating from reasoning to dialogue.
Table 14: Operational definitions of Schwartz values used for annotation in ValueJudge.
Table 9: (Left) Mapping from fine-grained topics to broad categories. (Right) Distribution of major topics in the dataset.
Figure 1: Overview of VALDI framework. We generate the DAISY dataset. Then we generate Fast and Slow Dialogue. Finally, we evaluate model’s dialogue and reasoning via our alignment metrics.

Figure 4: Overview of VIVALDI-D’s multi-agent dialogue auditor. We create a system with ValueExtractor to evaluate distorted values, rubricate them, and make a plan to correct the output. VIVALDI-R&D performs iterative reasoning-level repair to improve alignment, and applies dialoguelevel repair when alignment fails to propagate to the final output.
Table 15: VIVALDI-D hyperparameters.

Table 28: Per-value macro F1 alignment rates for Fast and Slow generation with Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite on the 500-scenario subset. Confidence intervals represent 95% bootstrap confidence intervals over scenarios.
Table 27: Stated value inclination distribution for Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite on the 500-scenario subset.

Table 19: Per-value macro F1 alignment rates for Fast and Slow generation on GPT-4o across the full DAISY dataset. Confidence intervals represent 95% bootstrap confidence intervals computed over scenarios. Most values exhibit reduced alignment under deliberative reasoning, with especially large declines for Stimulation, Achievement, Security, and Universalism.
Table 18: Full prompts used in VIVALDI-R and VIVALDI-R&D for dialogue-level planning and rewriting.

Table 20: Distribution of stated value inclinations in Task 1 across the DAISY dataset for GPT-4o. Values indicate the proportion of scenarios in which the model discourages, remains neutral toward, or supports each Schwartz value.

Table 21: Reasoning-to-dialogue transition statistics across values. Survival indicates values retained from reasoning into final dialogue, suppression indicates values present in reasoning but absent in dialogue, and emergence indicates values introduced in dialogue without appearing in reasoning. McNemar exact tests show that suppression significantly exceeds emergence across all values.
Table 6: Evaluation criteria used to assess generated reflections and paraphrased scenarios, adapted from prior work on response quality and paraphrase evaluation [35, 36].
Table 17: Full prompts used in VIVALDI-D for dialogue-level planning and rewriting. Prompts are shown exactly as implemented, excluding scenario-specific variables and repeated value definitions.

Figure 5: Per-value macro F1 alignment across Fast (T2), Slow (T3), and VIVALDI variants. Dialoguelevel intervention (VIVALDI-D) consistently improves alignment over both baselines across most values, while reasoning-only intervention (VIVALDI-R) yields smaller gains. Increasing the rewrite budget (t=1 → t=2) provides modest improvements for select values, suggesting diminishing returns from additional iterations.
Table 23: Stated value inclination distribution for LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct on the 500-scenario subset.
Table 26: Per-value macro F1 alignment rates for Fast and Slow generation with Qwen3-8B on the 500-scenario subset. Confidence intervals represent 95% bootstrap confidence intervals over scenarios.
Table 25: Stated value inclination distribution for Qwen3-8B on the 500-scenario subset.

Table 24: Per-value macro F1 alignment rates for Fast and Slow generation with LLaMA-3.1-8BInstruct on the 500-scenario subset. Confidence intervals represent 95% bootstrap confidence intervals over scenarios.
Table 16: VIVALDI-R and VIVALDI R&D hyperparameters.
Figure 2: Comparison of value–action alignment across Fast and Slow thinking with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals. We observe that deliberative reasoning reduces alignment.
